description: Browser Group Policy registry paths used by adware to force-install extensions, override homepages, and control search engines. These are set via HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies and bypass the browser's own UI.
note: IMPORTANT: Legitimate enterprise IT deployments also use these keys. JRTreborn only flags ExtensionInstallForcelist entries that match known malicious extension IDs, and Homepage/Search policy keys that are set without matching any known browser management software.

suspicious_policy_values.name: Chrome Homepage Override
suspicious_policy_values.description: Locks the Chrome homepage — commonly set by browser hijackers

suspicious_policy_values.name: Chrome Homepage Override (Wow64)
suspicious_policy_values.description: Locks the Chrome homepage (32-bit policy on 64-bit OS)

suspicious_policy_values.name: Chrome New Tab Page Override
suspicious_policy_values.description: Overrides the Chrome new tab page

suspicious_policy_values.name: Chrome Search Provider URL
suspicious_policy_values.description: Overrides the default search engine search URL

suspicious_policy_values.name: Chrome Search Provider Name
suspicious_policy_values.description: Overrides the default search engine name

suspicious_policy_values.name: Chrome Restore On Startup
suspicious_policy_values.description: Forces a specific page on startup (value 4 = open specific URLs)

suspicious_policy_values.name: Edge New Tab Page Override
suspicious_policy_values.description: Overrides the Edge new tab page

suspicious_policy_values.name: Edge Search Provider URL
suspicious_policy_values.description: Overrides the default search engine in Edge

suspicious_policy_values.name: Edge Restore On Startup
suspicious_policy_values.description: Forces a specific page on startup in Edge

force_install_extension_keys.name: Chrome Force-Install Extensions
force_install_extension_keys.description: Force-installed Chrome extensions — adware uses this to permanently install hijack extensions
